title: daphne sophia subsp. taurica
intro: Daphne sophia subsp. taurica, also known as the Winter Daphne or Daphne taurica, is a plant species in the family Thymelaeaceae. It is a shrub native to the Caucasus region with fragrant, white or pale pink flowers that bloom in winter. The plant is used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Daphne sophia subsp. taurica include its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, and its use in herbal preparations like teas, infusions, and tinctures.
